engineCodes.h2: Vauxhall Engine Codes & Average Rebuilt Prices (UK Supply)
engineCodes.h3: Compare costs for every major Vauxhall engine — Family 0 (Small Petrol), Family Z (Diesel), and the high‑failure 1.5 BlueHDi DV5 range. All prices are typical UK market ranges for professionally rebuilt engines with warranty. Click "Get Quote" on any engine to receive tailored prices from our network of UK Vauxhall specialists.

groups.name: Family 0 / Small Petrol Engines
groups.failureNote: The “Family 0” Ecotec engines power most small Vauxhall petrol cars. These are interference designs where timing chain failure often causes bent valves and catastrophic engine damage. Regular oil changes are critical. Common failure points: The Family 0 engines are known for timing chain stretch and rattle, especially when oil changes are neglected. Oil filter collapse can starve the chain of lubrication — a stretched chain leads to jumped timing and piston‑to‑valve contact. Our rebuilt units include a complete timing chain kit, upgraded chain tensioner, and new oil pump.

engines.compatibleModels: Astra J, Corsa D, Mokka, Meriva B

groups.name: Family Z / Diesel Engines
groups.failureNote: The Family Z diesel range (Z17DTH, Z19DTH, Z19DT, Z19DTL) has powered thousands of Vauxhall vans and passenger cars. These engines are sensitive to fuel system and intake carbon buildup. Common failure points: Family Z diesels suffer from dosing unit (fuel pressure regulator) contamination, which causes rough running, hard starting, and P0190 fault codes . The Z19DTH also has timing chain issues. Our rebuilt units include a new dosing unit, cleaned injectors, and a full timing chain kit.

engines.compatibleModels: Astra H, Vectra C, Zafira B
Astra H, Vectra C, Zafira B, Signum

groups.name: 1.6 CDTi / B16DTH
groups.failureNote: The B16DTH (and related R9M) diesel engines are fitted across thousands of Vauxhall Astras, Cascadas, and Zafiras. They are prone to DPF clogging, oil pump chain failure, and injector problems. Common failure points: The B16DTH is notorious for DPF regeneration issues and injector failure . Oil pump chain failure can write off the engine. Our rebuilt units include a new oil pump chain, reconditioned injectors, and a professionally cleaned DPF.

engines.compatibleModels: Vivaro B, Combo D, Movano B

groups.name: 2.0 CDTi / B20DTH
groups.failureNote: The B20DTH and related LFS engines (2015–present) are fitted in newer Insignias, Grandland Xs, and Vivaro vans. High‑pressure fuel pump (HPFP) failure is catastrophic and often destroys the entire fuel system. Common failure points: The B20DTH’s high‑pressure fuel pump (HPFP) can shed metal debris, contaminating injectors, fuel rails, and the entire system — a total engine replacement is often the only fix. Our rebuilt units include a new HPFP and a fully flushed fuel system.

engines.compatibleModels: Insignia B, Grandland X, Zafira C

groups.name: 1.5 BlueHDi / DV5 Diesel
groups.failureNote: The 1.5 BlueHDi (DV5) engines replaced the 1.6 CDTi in many models. While Euro 6‑compliant, they suffer from AdBlue system failures, EGR clogging, and injector problems . Common failure points: The 1.5 BlueHDi engines are known for AdBlue system crystallisation, EGR valve clogging, and DPF back‑pressure issues. Our rebuilt units include a fully decarbonised EGR system and a reconditioned AdBlue injector.

engines.compatibleModels: Astra K, Crossland X, Grandland X

groups.name: 2.2 / Z22SE Petrol
groups.failureNote: The Z22SE is a 2.2L naturally aspirated petrol engine found in older Vectras and Zafiras. Cylinder head cracking, head gasket failure, and timing chain stretch are common issues . Common failure points: The Z22SE suffers from porous cylinder heads, head gasket failure (white smoke, coolant loss), and a weak timing chain. Our rebuilt units include a pressure‑tested replacement head and a new timing chain kit.

groups.name: 1.6 Turbo / A16XER
groups.failureNote: A 1.6L naturally aspirated petrol engine found in mid‑size Vauxhalls. Oil consumption and timing chain stretch are the primary failure modes. Common failure points: The A16XER suffers from timing chain stretch and excessive oil consumption. Using the correct grade oil is critical. Our rebuilt units include a new timing chain kit and refurbished piston rings

engines.compatibleModels: Astra J, Zafira C, Meriva B

commonProblems.h2: Common Vauxhall Engine Problems — What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: Vauxhall engines are overwhelmingly value-driven, but high-volume failure modes like timing chain stretch on the 1.2L (A12XER) and 2.0 CDTI (Z19DTH) dominate replacement demand. Diesel system complexities on the 1.6 CDTI (B16DTH) and the "Hot-V" crisis on the 2.0 CDTI (B20DTH/LFS) mean many owners face a repair-or-replace decision earlier than expected. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point — plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this decision tool to compare realistic UK costs for each major failure type.

problems.h4: Timing Chain Failure – Why Vauxhall Petrol & Diesel Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Vauxhall Corsa, Astra, Meriva (2006–2014, A12XER 1.2L petrol); Vauxhall Astra, Zafira, Insignia (2004–2014, Z19DTH 1.9 CDTI). Also common on A14NET, B16DTH, and Z22SE engines.
problems.rootCause: Over-extended service intervals and a poorly tensioned chain guide system cause the timing chain to stretch. If ignored, the chain jumps teeth, valves collide with pistons, and the engine catastrophically fails.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Replacement of timing chain, tensioners, guides, and camshaft sprockets. Full oil and filter change. No cylinder head work required. | 60,000–80,000 miles.
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if caught early with no valve or piston damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Cylinder head removal, valve inspection/replacement, chain kit, water pump, and full gasket set. | 80,000+ miles. Required if timing has already slipped.

| Supply & fit of a rebuilt engine with upgraded timing components, bench tested, 12‑month warranty, new oil, filters, and coolant. | 100,000+ mile expectancy. Best value when engine has already suffered valve damage.

problems.recommendation: On a Vauxhall worth £1,500–£3,500, the minor chain replacement is your best-value option if caught early. If you hear a rattling noise on cold start, act immediately — ignoring it will double the repair bill. For engines already experiencing misfires or non-start, the specialist replacement route is cheaper than a dealer top-end rebuild.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt A12XER and Z19DTH engine prices for your Corsa or Astra

problems.h4: 2.0 CDTI High‑Pressure Pump (HPFP) Crisis – The Insignia & Vivaro Fuel System Destroyer
problems.affectedModels: Vauxhall Insignia (2014–2018, B20DTH/LFS 2.0 CDTI); Vauxhall Vivaro (2014–2019, 2.0 CDTI)
problems.rootCause: The Bosch CP4 high‑pressure fuel pump sheds metal particles directly into the fuel system. These particles destroy injectors, the fuel rail, and the pump itself — contamination reaches the engine via the return line, causing bore scoring and ring seizure.

repairOptions.tier: | Intermediate – HPFP + Full Fuel System Flush
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Replacement HPFP, full fuel system chemical flush (tank to rail), new fuel filter, injector cleaning/inspection. | Suitable only if no metal debris entered the engine. Requires immediate action at first misfire.

repairOptions.tier: | Full Replacement – Rebuilt Engine + New Fuel System
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Supply & fit of a rebuilt engine (uprated bearings, new injectors, HPFP, fuel rail, tank flush). 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. | The only reliable solution if the engine already runs rough or knocks. Metal debris in the bores always leads to rapid failure.

problems.recommendation: The B20DTH HPFP failure is a system‑wide contamination event. Do not attempt a partial repair — if the engine has run rough for more than 50 miles, the only sensible route is a rebuilt engine with a complete new fuel system. Specialist supply & fit is typically £1,500 cheaper than dealer pricing for the same work.
problems.cta: → Get fixed‑price quotes for rebuilt B20DTH engines with new HPFP & injectors

problems.h4: 1.6 CDTI (B16DTH) DPF & EGR System Failures – The Van Killer
problems.affectedModels: Vauxhall Astra (2014–2018), Vauxhall Zafira (2014–2018), Vauxhall Vivaro & Combo (2014–2020, B16DTH 1.6 CDTI)
problems.rootCause: Excessive short‑journey driving combined with a poorly calibrated DPF regeneration strategy causes soot overload. The EGR valve sticks open, recirculating exhaust gases continuously, which kills boost and eventually seizes the turbo.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Forced DPF regeneration (off‑vehicle clean), new EGR valve, intake manifold cleaning, diagnostic reset. | 30,000–40,000 miles.
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if no turbo damage and engine still starts/runs without major smoke.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | New turbo cartridge, DPF, EGR valve, oil cooler flush, full oil change, and ECU software update to improve regen strategy. | 80,000 miles. Required if turbo has lost boost or excessive oil has passed through the intercooler.

| Supply & fit of a rebuilt B16DTH with uprated turbo oil feed, cleaned intake system, new timing kit, 12‑month warranty. | Best for vans and high‑mileage fleet vehicles where the original engine has suffered multiple DPF blockages.

problems.recommendation: For a Vivaro or Combo used for delivery work, the minor DPF clean + EGR replacement is a short‑term fix. If you've seen the engine management light twice in six months, go straight to the rebuilt engine with updated ECU software — it's cheaper than a second turbo failure six months later.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt B16DTH engine prices for your Vivaro, Combo, or Astra

problems.h4: 1.4 Turbo (A14NET) Piston Ring & Oil Consumption – The Mokka & Astra Premature Fader
problems.affectedModels: Vauxhall Mokka (2012–2016), Vauxhall Astra J (2010–2015), Vauxhall Cascada (2013–2018) — all with A14NET 1.4T
problems.rootCause: Carbon buildup on piston rings due to insufficient oil control and a crankcase ventilation system that allows oil mist into the intake. Rings seize, blow‑by increases, and oil consumption reaches 1 litre per 500 miles.

repairOptions.tier: | Minor – Piston Soak & PCV System Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Chemical piston ring soak, new PCV valve, oil change, 10W‑40 high‑viscosity oil fill, and ECU oil consumption adaptation. | 10,000–15,000 miles. Short‑term relief only.
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if you're selling the car within 6 months.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Strip engine, replace piston rings, hone bores, new crankcase breather system, timing chain kit, turbo oil drain clean. | 60,000+ miles. The only proper fix. Requires engine‑out work.

| Supply & fit of a rebuilt A14NET with improved low‑tension rings, upgraded PCV system, new turbo, 12‑month warranty. | Best value if the engine already misfires, fails emissions, or consumes oil heavily.

problems.recommendation: The minor soak is not a permanent fix — it only buys time. On a Mokka or Astra worth £2,000–£4,000, either commit to the major ring rebuild through a specialist (£1,500 range) or replace the vehicle. The rebuilt engine with uprated rings is over‑kill unless you plan to keep the car for 5+ years.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt A14NET engine prices for your Mokka or Astra

problems.h4: Engine Seizure After Timing Failure – The Total Loss Scenario
problems.affectedModels: All Vauxhall interference engines — particularly A12XER, Z19DTH, Z22SE, and B16DTH after timing chain or belt failure
problems.rootCause: Interference engine design means piston and valve occupy the same space at different times. When timing fails, they collide instantly, bending valves, cracking pistons, and often destroying the cylinder head and block.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Cylinder head strip, new valves/guides, piston replacement (one or two damaged cylinders), timing kit, head gasket set. | Only viable if bore walls are unscored and crank is true. For high‑value low‑mileage cars only.

repairOptions.tier: | Full Replacement – Reconditioned Short or Long Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Supply & fit of a fully reconditioned engine (bench tested, upgraded timing components, new oil pump, water pump, full gasket set). 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. | The standard recommendation. Almost always cheaper than a dealer top‑end rebuild after catastrophic failure.

problems.recommendation: Once a Vauxhall interference engine has seized due to timing failure, stop immediately — further cranking will destroy the bores. We almost never recommend the top‑end rebuild route unless the car is a low‑mileage collector example. A specialist supply & fit of a rebuilt engine with upgraded components is typically £800–£1,200 cheaper than a dealer attempting to salvage the original unit.
problems.cta: → Get instant quotes for reconditioned engines with upgraded timing components

engineTypes.h2: Vauxhall Engine Types – Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every Vauxhall engine type available through our UK network, with typical price ranges and recommendations based on your vehicle’s age, condition and your budget.

types.title: Vauxhall Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: A stripped, cleaned and fully inspected engine where all worn components are replaced with OEM‑spec parts. Best for owners who want factory reliability without the factory price – ideal for Vauxhall engines known for timing chain wear.
types.priceRange: Typical price range: £1,400 – £3,800 (depending on engine code)

types.title: Vauxhall Rebuilt Engines
types.description: A complete rebuild with upgraded components addressing known failure points – such as uprated timing chain kits, reinforced bearing shells, and new oil pumps – for a longer service life than a standard replacement. The recommended choice for A14NET, B16DTH, and B20DTH engines.

types.title: Used Vauxhall Engines
types.description: A tested, low‑mileage take‑out unit from a donor vehicle. The most affordable option when budget is the priority, with verified history and warranty available. Suitable for older Vauxhall models where full recon isn't economical.

types.title: Vauxhall Remanufactured Engines
types.description: A factory‑spec, as‑new engine: every surface is machined, every component is replaced, and the unit is built to meet exact OEM tolerances. The premium choice for Vauxhall performance models or owners wanting a zero‑mile engine.

types.title: Vauxhall Refurbished Engines
types.description: A lightly serviced, cleaned, and tested engine that works reliably but hasn't had a complete rebuild. Often a pragmatic, fast solution for older or budget‑sensitive Vauxhall models where a full recon isn't economical.

types.title: Vauxhall Supply & Fit Engines
types.description: Your replacement engine delivered and professionally installed by a UK Vauxhall specialist. Includes removal of the old unit, fitting, fluids, and a workmanship warranty. The stress‑free solution for owners without a trusted local garage.
types.priceRange: Typical added cost: £400 – £800 on top of engine price
types.cta: Find Vauxhall engine supply & fit deals

engineTypes.closing: All engine types include a minimum 12‑month warranty. Prices are typical UK market ranges and depend on exact engine code, supplier, and any additional fitting services. Use our free comparison tool to get a tailored quote for your Vauxhall model today.

engineSizes.h2: Vauxhall Engines by Size – Find the Right Displacement for Your Model
engineSizes.intro: Every Vauxhall engine size explained — with the models and engine codes that used each displacement — so you can quickly identify the correct replacement unit for your vehicle.

items.title: Vauxhall 1.3L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.3L diesel (Z13DT/DV4) was fitted across the value-focused Corsa, Combo, and Meriva models between 2005 and 2014. This GM-Multijet unit is known for fuel economy but suffers from DPF blockage (on later models) and injector seal leaks
items.cta: Get quotes for Vauxhall 1.3L diesel engines

items.title: Vauxhall 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5L BlueHDi (DV5) replaced the 1.6 CDTI from 2018, appearing in the Corsa F, Grandland X, and Combo. While cleaner and more efficient than older units, the DV5 engine suffers from AdBlue crystallisation issues and premature timing belt degradation
items.cta: Get quotes for Vauxhall 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: Vauxhall 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.6 CDTI (B16DTH/R9M) is one of Vauxhall's most common diesel engines, fitted to the Astra K, Zafira Tourer C, and Vivaro B. While more refined than the 1.9, this engine has two notorious failure clusters: DPF/EGR blockage in passenger cars and high-pressure pump failure in vans
items.cta: Get quotes for Vauxhall 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Vauxhall 1.7L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.7 CDTI (Z17DTH/A17DT) is an older-generation Isuzu-derived unit fitted to the Astra H, Zafira B, and Meriva. It's robust compared to later Fiat-derived units, but injector clamping issues and DMF failure are common
items.cta: Get quotes for Vauxhall 1.7L diesel engines

items.title: Vauxhall 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.9 CDTI (Z19DTH/DT) is the most failure-discussed Vauxhall diesel online. Fitted to the Astra H, Zafira B, Vectra C, and Signum, this Fiat-derived engine suffers from timing chain stretch, swirl flap ingestion, and DPF issues. Replacement demand remains very high
items.cta: Get quotes for Vauxhall 1.9L diesel engines

items.title: Vauxhall 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0 CDTI family covers three generations: the older Z20 (2004–2014), the A20 (2010–2016), and the B20 (2014–present). The B20DTH "Hot‑V" engine in the Insignia B and Vivaro C is the most failure-critical, with HPFP debris destroying the entire fuel system and engine. The A20DTH also suffers from timing chain issues
items.cta: Get quotes for Vauxhall 2.0L diesel engines
commonFailurePoints: B20DTH – HPFP failure (contaminates entire fuel system)

items.title: Vauxhall 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.2L DTi (Y22DTR) was Vauxhall's own in‑house diesel from the early 2000s, fitted to the Vectra C and Omega. It's rare today, but units that survive have usually dealt with injector loom failures
items.cta: Get quotes for Vauxhall 2.2L diesel engines
commonFailurePoints: Injector wiring loom failure inside rocker cover

items.title: Vauxhall 1.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.0L (A10XEP) three‑cylinder was introduced in the Corsa E and Adam as an eco‑friendly replacement for the 1.2. It's generally reliable, but the timing chain can stretch if oil changes are neglected
items.cta: Compare Vauxhall 1.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Vauxhall 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.2L family includes the older A12XER (timing chain) and the newer EB2 (1.2 PureTech turbo). The EB2 engine, fitted to the Corsa F, Grandland, and Mokka B, has a well‑documented wet‑belt degradation issue that can lead to oil starvation and catastrophic failure
items.cta: Compare Vauxhall 1.2L petrol engine prices
commonFailurePoints: EB2 – wet timing belt disintegration in oil

items.title: Vauxhall 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.4L family is Vauxhall's largest petrol volume segment. The naturally aspirated Z14XEP/A14XER is robust but has oil consumption issues. The turbocharged A14NET/B14NET (fitted to Astra J, Mokka A, Adam S) suffers from severe piston ring coking, leading to 1L/500 miles oil consumption and turbo failure
items.cta: Compare Vauxhall 1.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Vauxhall 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6L petrol covers three eras: the older Z16XEP/XER (timing chain issues), the turbo LDE/LHU (used in Insignia A and Astra J GTC, with piston and turbo failures), and the newer PureTech turbo (EB2-derived, with wet belt problems)
items.cta: Compare Vauxhall 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Vauxhall 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.8L (Z18XER) is a larger naturally aspirated unit used in Astra H, Zafira B, and Vectra C. It's one of Vauxhall's more reliable petrol engines, but timing chain stretch (40–80k miles) and oil consumption are common complaints
items.cta: Compare Vauxhall 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Vauxhall 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0L petrol is rare in later Vauxhalls but appears as the Z20LER/Z20LEH (turbo) in the Astra H VXR and Corsa D VXR. These high‑performance engines suffer from piston ring land failure (cylinder 3), timing chain stretch, and turbo failure
items.cta: Compare Vauxhall 2.0L petrol engine prices
commonFailurePoints: Piston ring failure on cylinder 3 (oil burning

items.title: Vauxhall 2.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.2L (Z22SE) fitted to the Vectra C, Signum, and Zafira A/B is an older GM Ecotec unit. Timing chain stretch is the dominant failure – if the chain jumps, the interference engine self‑destroys. Replacement demand remains steady
items.cta: Compare Vauxhall 2.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Vauxhall 2.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.8L V6 Turbo (B284) was fitted to the Insignia VXR and Vectra VXR. It's a strong engine but suffers from timing chain tensioner failure and PCV/ breather system clogging
items.cta: Compare Vauxhall 2.8L petrol engine prices
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner failure (catastrophic if ignored)
breather system clogging (rear main seal failure)

items.title: Vauxhall Electric Motors
items.description: Vauxhall's fully electric range (Corsa-e, Mokka-e, Combo-e, Vivaro-e) uses the same PSA-derived permanent magnet synchronous motor (136–168 bhp). These motors are highly reliable, but failure is rare and typically related to the on-board charger or bearing noise, not the motor windings. Motor code(s): EMotors (PSA/Stellantis EV platform)
items.cta: Enquire about Vauxhall electric motor repair

items.title: Vauxhall Diesel Engines
items.description: Vauxhall diesel engines cover many of the brand's highest-mileage UK models. Replacement demand is usually driven by timing system wear, injector and turbo faults, plus EGR or DPF-related damage once repeat short-journey use starts affecting reliability.
knownFor: DPF clogging (2010-on)
injector seal leakage
EGR valve sticking
AdBlue tank/sensor failure
importantNotes: Short-journey use increases DPF, EGR, and oil-dilution risk on many diesel engines.
A documented service history matters more on diesel replacements because injector, turbo, and timing faults often build gradually.
items.cta: Compare Vauxhall diesel engine prices

items.title: Vauxhall Petrol Engines
items.description: Vauxhall petrol engines span everything from core daily-driver models to higher-output performance variants. Most replacement enquiries start with timing chain noise, oil consumption, cooling-system failure, or internal wear that makes a warranted replacement more sensible than repeated repairs.
knownFor: Timing chain stretch
oil consumption (piston rings)
A12XER – timing chain stretch (40-70k miles)
EB2 – wet timing belt disintegration in oil
importantNotes: Cold-start rattles, overheating history, and oil use are the biggest warning signs before a petrol engine replacement becomes necessary.
Direct-injection petrol engines often need specialist attention for carbon build-up alongside the main mechanical repair.

engineCodeDirectory.h2: Vauxhall Engine Codes – Most Replaced Engines (2008–2025)
engineCodeDirectory.intro: Every major Vauxhall engine code from the last two decades, grouped by family, with compatible models and typical reasons why owners seek replacement. Select your engine to compare rebuilt prices and get quotes from UK Vauxhall specialists.

entries.title: A14NET 1.4L Turbo Petrol
entries.description: The A14NET (138bhp) powers the Astra J, Corsa D, Mokka A, and Meriva B (2009–2015). Timing chain stretch is the most common failure – the chain tensioner fails, the chain jumps, and the interference engine bends valves. Piston ring damage causes excessive oil consumption (1L per 1,000 miles). Turbocharger failure (wastegate rattle, oil seal leakage) is also frequent. Our rebuilt units include an upgraded timing chain kit, new piston rings, and a reconditioned turbo.
entries.cta: Get quotes for A14NET Vauxhall engine replacement

entries.title: A12XER 1.2L Petrol
entries.description: Found in the Corsa D and Astra H (2008–2014, 84bhp). Timing chain stretch usually occurs between 60,000–80,000 miles, often leading to a non-starting engine with bent valves. The chain tensioner is weak, and oil filter collapse can starve the chain of lubrication. Our rebuilt units include a complete timing chain kit with an upgraded tensioner and a new oil pump.

entries.title: Z16XER / A16XER 1.6L Petrol
entries.description: These 1.6L naturally aspirated engines (113–115bhp) equipped the Astra H/J, Zafira B, and Meriva B (2005–2014). Timing chain stretch and excessive oil consumption (stuck piston rings) are the primary failure points. Coil pack failure and camshaft sensor faults are also common. Our rebuilt units include an uprated timing chain kit and refurbished piston rings to address oil burning.
entries.cta: Get quotes for Z16XER / A16XER Vauxhall engine replacement

entries.title: Z22SE 2.2L Petrol
entries.description: The Z22SE (147bhp) powered the Vectra C, Signum, and Zafira B (2002–2007). Porous cylinder heads cause head gasket failure with white smoke and coolant loss. Timing chain stretch and tensioner failure are also common – a snapped chain destroys the valves. Our rebuilt units include a pressure-tested replacement head and a new timing chain kit.

entries.title: Z19DTH / Z19DT 1.9L Diesel
entries.description: The Z19DTH (148–150bhp) and Z19DT (118bhp) were fitted to Astra H, Vectra C, Zafira B, and Signum (2004–2010). Dosing unit (fuel pressure regulator) failure allows diesel to contaminate the engine oil, thinning it and causing main bearing and crankshaft seizure. Timing chain stretch and DPF clogging (on later variants) are also common. Our rebuilt units include a new dosing unit, a full timing chain kit, and a professionally cleaned DPF.
entries.cta: Get quotes for Z19DTH Vauxhall diesel engine replacement

entries.title: B16DTH / R9M 1.6L Diesel
entries.description: The B16DTH (134bhp) and R9M (118–138bhp) power the Astra K, Cascada, Zafira C, Vivaro B, and Combo D (2014–2020). DPF regeneration issues lead to soot buildup, back-pressure, and eventual DPF meltdown. Injector failure causes white smoke, misfires, and oil dilution. Oil pump chain failure is a sudden, catastrophic failure with no warning. Our rebuilt units include a new oil pump chain, four reconditioned injectors, and a professionally cleaned DPF.
entries.cta: Get quotes for B16DTH / R9M Vauxhall engine replacement

entries.title: B20DTH / LFS 2.0L Diesel
entries.description: The B20DTH (168bhp) and LFS (118–168bhp) equip Insignia B, Grandland X, Zafira C, and Vivaro C (2015–present). High-pressure fuel pump (HPFP) disintegration sends metal debris through the entire fuel system – injectors, fuel rail, lines, and tank. The debris often bypasses the filter and scores cylinder bores and camshaft journals. A full fuel system replacement plus a rebuilt engine is the only reliable fix. Our rebuilt units include a new HPFP, a full fuel system replacement, and a thoroughly flushed oil system.
entries.cta: Compare B20DTH / LFS Vauxhall engine prices

entries.title: DV5RC / DV5RCE 1.5L Diesel
entries.description: The 1.5 BlueHDi DV5 engines (99–130bhp) are found in Astra K, Corsa F, Mokka B, Crossland X, and Vivaro C (2018–present). AdBlue system crystallisation (NOx sensors, injectors) and EGR valve clogging are the dominant failures. DPF back-pressure issues occur with repeated short journeys. Our rebuilt units include a decarbonised EGR system, a reconditioned AdBlue injector, and a professionally cleaned DPF.
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV5 1.5 BlueHDi Vauxhall engine replacement

entries.title: EB2ADTS 1.2L Turbo Petrol
entries.description: The EB2ADTS (108–128bhp) wet belt engine powers Corsa F, Mokka B, Grandland X, and Crossland X (2018–present). The timing belt runs inside the engine oil and degrades, shedding rubber particles that block the oil pickup strainer. Oil starvation follows, destroying the turbo and crankshaft bearings – often by 50,000 miles. Our rebuilt units include a new wet belt kit, a new oil pump, and a rebuilt turbocharger.
entries.cta: Compare EB2ADTS 1.2 Turbo Vauxhall engine prices

entries.title: B284 / A28NET 2.8L V6 Turbo Petrol
entries.description: The 2.8 V6 Turbo (B284, A28NET, Z28NET – 247–321bhp) powered the Insignia VXR and Vectra VXR (2005–2012). Turbocharger failure (wastegate rattle, oil seals) is common, as is timing chain stretch and oil cooler leaks. Replacement engines are expensive – our network supplies remanufactured units with upgraded turbo components and a new timing chain kit.
entries.cta: Get quotes for B284 / A28NET VXR Vauxhall engine replacement

engineYears.h2: Vauxhall Engines by Year – What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers every major Vauxhall engine change across all UK-market models from the late 1990s to the present, organised by year so owners can identify their exact engine family, understand when key powertrains were introduced or retired, and compare rebuilt or replacement engine prices from UK Vauxhall specialists.

years.preview: Ecotec Family Dominant: X-Series Across the Range
years.description: The late 1990s saw Vauxhall's GM-Opel Ecotec family firmly established across every model line - the X16XE, X18XE, X20XEV, and Z22SE petrol units alongside the X17DTL and X20DTH diesels powering one of the UK's highest-volume car ranges.
keyChanges: Vauxhall Astra G (1998) launches with X-series Ecotec petrol range; X16XE 1.6 and X18XE 1.8 as primary petrol options
Vectra B continues with X18XE, X20XEV 2.0, and Z22SE 2.2 units; diesel range uses X20DTH 2.0 turbodiesel
Zafira A (1999) brings the 1.8-litre Ecotec into MPV duties alongside the unique 2.0 OPC Turbo for the VXR predecessor
Omega V6 2.5/3.0 KV6 engines offer Vauxhall's only V6 in mainstream range; X25XE/X30XE for Omega and Carlton
Y-series upgrade begins rolling across Corsa and Astra from 2000 - incremental emissions improvements toward Euro 3
mainEngines: X16XE / Z16XE - 1,598cc DOHC 16v (Astra G, Corsa B/C, Vectra B)
X18XE / Z18XE - 1,796cc DOHC 16v (Astra G, Vectra B, Zafira A, Omega)
X20XEV / Z22SE - 1,998–2,198cc DOHC (Vectra B 2.0/2.2, Astra G 2.0, Omega)
X20DTH / Y20DTH - 1,994cc turbodiesel (Vectra B 2.0 DTi, Astra G diesel)
X25XE / X30XE - 2,498–2,962cc KV6 (Omega V6)
knownFor: X-series Ecotec petrol engines are among the most reliable units Vauxhall ever produced - naturally aspirated architecture, robust bottom ends, and an enormous parts supply across the GM network
X20DTH 2.0 turbodiesel in the Vectra and Astra is a proven workhorse but prone to EGR valve blockage and injector seal leaks from 80,000 miles onward, particularly on high-urban-mileage examples
Omega KV6 is a smooth, characterful engine let down by the complexity of its ancillaries - coolant pipe failure and water pump issues are the primary specialist callouts
Timing belt-driven X-series units require strict belt change intervals at 40,000 miles - catastrophic engine damage on failure is well documented across all models using this family
years.cta: Compare 1998–2001 Vauxhall Ecotec replacement engine prices from UK specialists

years.preview: Vectra C, Z-Series & 1.9 CDTi Preview Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The Vectra C (2002) and third-generation Astra H preview (2004) brought the refined Z-series Ecotec petrol family and introduced the 2.2 direct injection DTi diesel - transitional units between the old X-series and the landmark 1.9 CDTI that would follow.
keyChanges: Vectra C (2002) launches with Z18XE and Z22SE petrol plus Z20DTH/Z20DTJ diesel options
Signum (2003) shares Vectra C platform and same engine range; adds 3.2 V6 Z32SE for flagship
Astra H (2004) arrives with Z16XER and Z18XER revised DOHC Ecotec units - improved breathing, revised valve timing
Z16XER/Z18XER introduced with revised camshaft half-moon design; later-production units (post Nov 2008) address known cranking/sensor issue
Euro 3 compliance standard across range; Euro 4 preparation underway for 2005 onwards
mainEngines: Z16XER - 1,598cc DOHC 16v VVT (Astra H, Zafira B)
Z18XER - 1,796cc DOHC 16v VVT (Astra H, Vectra C, Zafira B, Meriva A)
Z22SE / Z22YH - 2,198cc DOHC (Vectra C, Signum 2.2)
Z32SE - 3,175cc DOHC V6 (Vectra C V6, Signum V6)
Z20DTH / Z20DTJ - 1,994cc turbodiesel (Vectra C, Signum diesel)
knownFor: Z18XER is a broadly reliable unit across the Astra H and Vectra C, but camshaft half-moon clearance is a documented production concern - P0340/P0365 no-start fault codes are the symptom; confirmed fixed in production from November 2008 build dates
Camshaft bearing bridge oil leaks are a separate, ongoing Z16XER/Z18XER service item - common from 60,000 miles onward; correct resealing with fresh compound is the remedy
Z22SE continues the robust character of the wider Ecotec family; direct injection on the Z22YH variant demands more diligent oil-change intervals to prevent GDI carbon build-up
Z20DTH diesel in the Vectra C begins showing early signs of EGR and swirl flap issues that would become much more pronounced in the 1.9 CDTI generation
years.cta: Get quotes for 2002–2004 Vauxhall Vectra, Astra and Signum replacement engines from UK specialists

years.preview: 1.9 CDTI Dominates; VXR Arrives Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The 2.0-litre Fiat/GM co-developed 1.9 CDTI (Z19DTH/Z19DT) became Vauxhall's dominant diesel and one of the most complained-about engines of the 2000s - powerful, economical, and widespread, but plagued by swirl flap failure and injector corrosion that generate the highest replacement enquiry volumes of any Vauxhall diesel era.
keyChanges: 1.9 CDTI (Z19DTH 150bhp, Z19DT 120bhp, Z19DTL 100bhp) introduced across Astra H, Vectra C, Zafira B, Signum - the brand's primary diesel offer
Astra H VXR (2005) debuts with Z20LEH 2.0 turbo petrol (240bhp) - Vauxhall's first hot hatchback engine of the modern era
Corsa C / Corsa D (2006) transitions to Z10XE 1.0 and Z12XE/Z14XEP 1.4 petrol; 1.3 CDTi (Z13DTH) introduced as entry diesel
Euro 4 compliance achieved across the entire range by 2006 MY
Z18XER and Z16XER continue across Astra H, Zafira B, and Meriva A
mainEngines: Z19DTH - 1,910cc CDTI turbodiesel 150bhp (Astra H, Vectra C, Zafira B, Signum)
Z19DT / Z19DTL - 1,910cc CDTI 100–120bhp (Astra H, Corsa D, Meriva)
Z20LEH - 1,998cc turbocharged petrol 240bhp (Astra H VXR)
Z18XER / Z16XER - 1,796/1,598cc DOHC VVT petrol (Astra H, Vectra C, Zafira B)
Z13DTH - 1,248cc CDTI (Corsa D 1.3 diesel, Combo)
Z14XEP / Z16XE1 - 1,364/1,598cc (Corsa D, Meriva)
knownFor: 1.9 CDTI swirl flap failure is the single most documented Vauxhall engine problem of the decade - sticking or broken flaps in the intake manifold cause loss of power, P1109/P2279 fault codes, and if debris enters the combustion chamber, catastrophic engine damage

Injector plug fretting corrosion on Z19DTH causes misfires and P0201–P0204 codes - factory-issued ECU remap and modified wiring harness is the known fix, but many high-mileage cars carry this fault unresolved
EGR valve clogging with soot deposits is practically universal on urban-duty 1.9 CDTI examples - accelerated by short-journey use and extended service intervals
Astra VXR Z20LEH is an entertaining but maintenance-intensive unit - turbo oil feed pipe blockage leads to premature turbocharger failure if oil changes are neglected beyond 6,000-mile intervals

years.preview: Insignia Launch: A20DTH & 2.0T Ecotec Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The all-new Insignia (2008) introduced the A-series Ecotec engine family - the 2.0 CDTI A20DTH and 2.0 turbo petrol A20NFT - replacing the Z-series across the mainstream mid-size range and representing Vauxhall's most comprehensive engine modernisation in a decade.
keyChanges: Insignia A (2008) launches with A20DTH 2.0 CDTI (130/160bhp), A20NHT 2.0 turbo petrol (220bhp), and A18XER 1.8 petrol
A-series codenames replace Z-series across new and refreshed models - A14XER 1.4, A16XER 1.6, A18XER 1.8 petrol; A20DTH 2.0 diesel
Astra H refreshed mid-cycle with A-series engine introductions alongside carry-over Z-series units
Insignia OPC (2009) brings A20NFT/A20NHT 2.0 turbo to 325bhp - the most powerful mainstream Vauxhall to that date
Euro 4 continues; Euro 5 preparation begins for 2010 MY
mainEngines: A20DTH - 1,956cc CDTI turbodiesel 130–160bhp (Insignia, Astra H late, Zafira B late)
A20NHT - 1,998cc turbopetrol 220bhp (Insignia 2.0T)
A20NFT - 1,998cc turbopetrol 325bhp (Insignia OPC)
A18XER - 1,796cc DOHC VVT (Insignia 1.8, Astra H late)
A14XER / A16XER - 1,364–1,598cc SOHC (Corsa D, Meriva, Astra H late)
knownFor: A20DTH oil pickup seal deterioration is the Insignia's most serious known mechanical fault - hardened seal allows air ingestion into the oil circuit, causing low oil pressure warnings and ultimately bearing damage if not addressed proactively
EGR cooler cover cracking is a secondary A20DTH issue - coolant leaks into the exhaust circuit; inexpensive part but requires timely attention to prevent hydrolock risk
A20DTH is noisy on cold start by modern standards - characteristic clatter is normal for the architecture but if clattering persists at operating temperature, bottom-end investigation is warranted
A20NHT 2.0 turbo petrol in the Insignia is a strong, well-liked unit overall - DPF-free, responsive, and relatively straightforward for UK specialists to rebuild

years.cta: Get quotes for 2008–2010 Vauxhall Insignia and Astra A-series replacement engines from UK specialists

years.preview: Astra J, 1.6 CDTI Whisper & 1.4 Turbo Era Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The Astra J (2010) brought the acclaimed 1.6 CDTI "Whisper" diesel and the 1.4 turbo petrol into mainstream UK ownership - two genuinely new engine architectures that defined Vauxhall's efficiency agenda through the decade but carried their own well-documented reliability considerations.
keyChanges: Astra J (2010) launches with A14NET/A14NEL 1.4 turbo petrol (100–140bhp) and A16LET 1.6 turbo; A20NFT 2.0T for VXR
A16DTH / B16DTH 1.6 CDTI "Whisper" diesel introduced from 2013 across Astra J K and Insignia - Euro 6 capable, notably quieter than the 2.0 CDTI it supplements
A20DTH 2.0 CDTI continues in Insignia Sports Tourer and higher-output variants
Mokka (2012) and Adam (2012) join range with 1.4 petrol (A14NET) and 1.3 CDTI diesel (A13FDH)
Euro 5 compliance standard across the range from 2011 MY
mainEngines: A14NET / A14NEL - 1,364cc turbocharged petrol 100–140bhp (Astra J, Corsa D, Mokka, Adam, Meriva B)
A16LET - 1,598cc turbopetrol (Astra J Sport)
A16DTH / B16DTH - 1,598cc CDTI turbodiesel (Astra J K, Insignia, Mokka, Zafira Tourer)
A20DTH - 1,956cc CDTI continued (Insignia, Zafira Tourer 2.0 diesel)
A20NFT - 1,998cc turbo 280bhp (Astra J VXR)
knownFor: 1.4 turbo A14NET head gasket failure on early examples is the most discussed Vauxhall petrol engine issue of the decade - an ECU software update and oil spec revision (from 0W-30 to 5W-30) from Vauxhall addressed the worst cases, but unserviced pre-update examples remain at risk

PCV system failure on the A14NET causes unmetered air ingestion and lean running conditions - failed crankcase check valve is a well-documented independent specialist diagnosis, often misdiagnosed as a fuel fault

1.6 CDTI B16DTH timing chain tensioner drain-back fault is the engine's primary weakness - the OE tensioner lacks a non-return valve, causing start-up chain rattle and, if neglected, guide fragmentation with metal debris entering the sump
1.6 CDTI timing chain is mounted at the rear of the engine behind the gearbox - access requires gearbox removal, making replacement significantly more expensive than front-chain alternatives

years.preview: Astra K, B-Series Engines & Euro 6 Major Reliability Revision
years.description: The Astra K (2015) and Insignia B (2017) introduced the updated B-series engine family - revised 1.4 petrol, 1.6 CDTI with improved tensioner specification, and a new 1.5 CDTI diesel - while the GM/Vauxhall partnership with Opel continued to produce the highest-volume UK engine range.
keyChanges: Astra K (2015) launches with B14XFT/B14XFL 1.4 turbo (125–150bhp) - revised A14NET with improved cooling circuit and updated PCV system
B16DTH updated to address worst of timing chain tensioner drain-back - aftermarket tensioner with non-return valve becomes the de facto specialist fix for all generations
Insignia B (2017) introduces new 1.5 CDTI (D15DT) diesel replacing the ageing 1.6 CDTI; also gains 2.0 CDTI B20DTH for higher-output variants
Crossland X and Grandland X (2017) join range with 1.2 turbo petrol (B12XHT) and 1.6 CDTI (D16DTH) diesel
Full Euro 6 compliance required and achieved across the entire Vauxhall UK range
mainEngines: B14XFT / B14XFL - 1,364cc turbopetrol 100–150bhp (Astra K, Corsa E, Mokka X)
B16DTH (updated) - 1,598cc CDTI (Astra K, Insignia A late, Mokka X)
D15DT - 1,499cc CDTI turbodiesel (Insignia B 1.5 CDTI)
B20DTH - 1,956cc CDTI turbodiesel 170bhp (Insignia B 2.0 CDTI)
B12XHT - 1,199cc turbopetrol (Crossland X 1.2T)
knownFor: B14XFT is a measurable improvement over the A14NET - cooling system revisions reduce the head gasket risk significantly, but the PCV system and oil-change discipline remain the key variables for long-term reliability

1.6 CDTI tensioner drama continues into Astra K early production despite improvements - only the aftermarket drain-back-preventing tensioner reliably eliminates the cold start rattle and chain wear risk
Insignia B D15DT 1.5 CDTI inherits shared PSA/GM architecture concerns - EGR and DPF blockage on short-journey cars is the primary service consideration

B20DTH in the Insignia B carries forward the oil pickup seal awareness from the A20DTH - a preventative seal change before 60,000 miles remains the best-practice recommendation from independent specialists

years.preview: PSA Alliance: Corsa F & New Engine Architecture Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: Following PSA Group's acquisition of Vauxhall/Opel in 2017, the Corsa F (2019) became the first Vauxhall built on PSA's CMP platform - introducing entirely new French-sourced 1.2 PureTech turbo petrol and 1.5 BlueHDi diesel engines and definitively ending the GM Ecotec era.
keyChanges: Corsa F (2019) launches on PSA CMP platform - shares engines, structure, and platform with Peugeot 208
EB2ADTS / EB2ADTX 1.2 PureTech 3-cylinder turbo petrol (75–130bhp) replaces all previous 1.0/1.2/1.4 Ecotec petrol units
1.5 BlueHDi (DV5) diesel replaces 1.3 CDTI and 1.6 CDTI across Corsa and subsequently Crossland X
Corsa-e (2020) launched in parallel - first Vauxhall mainstream electric car; 50kWh/100kW (136PS) electric motor, 209 miles WLTP
All GM-era engine codes retired from the Corsa range entirely
mainEngines: EB2ADTS / EB2ADTX (PureTech 1.2T) - 1,199cc 3-cylinder turbopetrol 100–130bhp (Corsa F)
EB2ADE (PureTech 1.2 75PS) - 1,199cc naturally aspirated 3-cylinder (Corsa F entry)
DV5RD / DV5RC (BlueHDi 1.5) - 1,499cc 4-cylinder turbodiesel (Corsa F, Crossland X)
100kW electric motor / 50kWh battery (Corsa-e)
knownFor: PSA PureTech 1.2T is praised for refinement and economy but carries a well-publicised timing belt-in-oil failure mode on earlier variants - belt material degradation leads to catastrophic engine failure; PSA/Vauxhall extended warranties and replacement intervals have been issued
1.5 BlueHDi DPF failure on urban short-trip use is a known characteristic - Vauxhall issued extended special coverage for DPF issues to 10 years/150,000 miles for qualifying cars
Corsa-e marks a definitive landmark - the first volume-production electric Vauxhall; 50kWh battery shares architecture with Peugeot e-208, meaning parts supply is broad for an EV of this price
For UK independent specialists, the PSA engine transition requires new tooling and diagnostic capability - GM Ecotec expertise does not transfer directly to PureTech and BlueHDi servicing

years.preview: Astra L PHEV, Mokka Electric & Full PSA Integration Technology Introduction
years.description: The all-new Astra L (2022) and Mokka (2021) completed Vauxhall's platform migration to PSA (now Stellantis) - introducing the Astra PHEV 1.6 turbo hybrid, the Mokka-e BEV, and establishing the Stellantis STLA platform as the foundation for the brand's electrified future.
keyChanges: Mokka Mk2 (2021) launches on CMP platform - PSA 1.2 PureTech petrol, 1.5 BlueHDi diesel, and Mokka-e BEV (50kWh/136PS)
Astra L (2022) introduces PSA-sourced 1.2 PureTech 110/130bhp and 1.5 BlueHDi 130bhp alongside Astra Plug-in Hybrid (PHEV)
Astra PHEV (2022) pairs EP6 1.6 turbo petrol with 180bhp electric motor - combined 225bhp, 31-mile PHEV range
Grandland PHEV continues with 1.6 turbo + electric motor; up to 225bhp combined

DV5RD (BlueHDi 1.5) - 1,499cc diesel 130bhp (Astra L, Mokka, Grandland)

100kW / 136PS electric motor, 50kWh (Mokka-e, Corsa-e continuation)

PureTech 1.2T timing belt-in-oil concern carries forward into this generation - cars built after mid-2021 carry a revised belt specification, but earlier Astra L and Mokka owners should confirm belt status at every service

Mokka-e and Corsa-e BEV drivetrains have accumulated meaningful real-world mileage - early battery degradation data from UK owners suggests approximately 5–8% capacity loss by 50,000 miles under typical usage
Diesel Astra axed in 2024 - owners of 1.5 BlueHDi Astra L cars should factor in declining parts support trajectory when making engine repair vs. replacement decisions

years.preview: Corsa-e Refresh, Astra Electric & Stellantis BEV Era Technology Introduction
years.description: Vauxhall's current range is its most electrified in history - the Corsa-e, Mokka-e, and new Astra Electric (2024) are fully battery-powered, while petrol variants retain the PureTech 1.2T as the sole combustion choice as the brand targets full electrification by 2028.
keyChanges: Astra Electric (2024) arrives with 156bhp electric motor and 54kWh battery - 258-mile WLTP range; the first fully electric Astra
Corsa-e updated to 156PS motor option alongside the continued 136PS variant; same 50kWh battery platform

faq.h2: Vauxhall Engine Replacement – Frequently Asked Questions
faq.intro: ** Quick, honest answers to the most common questions Vauxhall owners ask before comparing engine replacement prices and finding a UK specialist.

items.question: How much does a Vauxhall engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A replacement Vauxhall engine typically costs between £1,200 and £2,800 for supply‑only on common models like the Corsa 1.2 (A12XER) or Astra 1.9 CDTI (Z19DTH). Reconditioned units with upgraded internals range from £1,800 to £3,500, while full supply and fit adds £400–£700 depending on the model and garage location. The final price varies significantly based on engine code, condition (used vs rebuilt), and whether the garage is a main dealer or independent specialist. For a precise quote tailored to your reg and engine code, use our free comparison tool.
items.cta: Compare Vauxhall engine replacement prices now

items.question: What affects the cost of a Vauxhall engine replacement?
items.answer: Four main factors drive the cost: engine size and complexity (a 1.2 Corsa engine is far cheaper than a 2.0 CDTI Insignia unit), condition (used engines cost less upfront but offer no failure prevention), vehicle age (older cars may require extra labour for seized bolts or corroded mounts), and supply‑only vs supply‑and‑fit. Location also matters – labour in London and the South East can be 20–30% higher than in the North or Midlands. Our comparison tool brings transparency by showing you fixed quotes from multiple Vauxhall specialists side‑by‑side.
items.cta: Get quotes from UK Vauxhall engine specialists

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Vauxhall?
items.answer: In most cases, yes – especially if the chassis, gearbox, and bodywork are in good condition. A rebuilt Vauxhall engine with upgraded components (e.g., a revised timing chain on a Z19DTH or uprated piston rings on an A14NET) often outlasts the original factory unit. For cars worth £1,500–£4,000, a specialist replacement at £1,500–£2,500 is usually more economical than buying another used vehicle with an unknown service history. If the car has extensive rust or transmission failure, scrapping may be the better option, but for most Vauxhall daily drivers, a quality replacement is the smart financial choice.
items.cta: Find reconditioned Vauxhall engines with comprehensive warranty cover

items.question: What's included in a Vauxhall engine supply and fit service?
items.answer: A full supply and fit package includes sourcing a matching engine (used, rebuilt, or reconditioned), removing the failed unit, transferring your existing ancillaries (injectors, turbo, manifolds), installing the replacement engine with new oil, filters, coolant, and spark plugs, plus a road test to verify performance. Most specialists also include a 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty on the engine itself and a 3–6 month labour warranty. Fitting costs typically range from £400–£700 for front‑wheel‑drive models like the Astra or Zafira, with vans like the Vivaro sometimes costing more due to tighter engine bays.
items.cta: Compare Vauxhall engine supply and fit deals

items.question: How long does a Vauxhall engine replacement take?
items.answer: For common models like the Corsa D, Astra J, or Zafira B, a straightforward like‑for‑like swap takes 3–5 working days. More complex models – particularly the Insignia B (B20DTH) or Vivaro, where engine access is restricted – can take 5–7 days. The single biggest variable is engine availability: standard codes like the A12XER or Z19DTH are usually in stock, while rarer units (e.g., Z22SE or B20DTH with specific revisions) may add 2–4 days.
items.cta: Get quotes and lead times from UK Vauxhall specialists

items.question: Do reconditioned Vauxhall engines come with warranty?
items.answer: Yes – every reconditioned and rebuilt Vauxhall engine supplied through EngineMarket comes with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty as standard. Many specialists also offer extended 24‑month coverage on premium rebuilds that include new timing chains, water pumps, piston rings, and bearings. The warranty typically covers major internal components (crankshaft, conrods, pistons, camshaft, timing gear) but excludes ancillaries like injectors, turbochargers, or the clutch unless explicitly stated. Always confirm the exact terms with your supplier, but a 12‑month parts and labour guarantee is the industry baseline for quality rebuilds.
items.cta: Compare Vauxhall engines with 12‑month warranty cover

items.question: Should I choose a used or reconditioned Vauxhall engine?
items.answer: It depends on your budget and how long you plan to keep the car. A used Vauxhall engine (pull‑and‑stripped) is cheaper upfront – typically £500–£1,200 – but it carries the same original design flaws (timing chain stretch, piston ring seizure) and offers no warranty beyond 30–90 days. A reconditioned engine (£1,500–£2,800) costs more but includes new wear parts, upgraded components addressing the original failure point, and a 12‑month minimum warranty. If you're selling the car within a year, a used engine can make sense. If you're keeping it, a reconditioned unit with upgraded parts is almost always better value.

items.question: What are the most common Vauxhall engine failures?
items.answer: The most common replacement‑driving failures are: timing chain stretch on the 1.9 CDTI (Z19DTH – Astra H, Zafira B) and 1.2 petrol (A12XER – Corsa D); high‑pressure fuel pump (HPFP) contamination on the 2.0 CDTI “Hot‑V” (B20DTH – Insignia B, Vivaro C); piston ring carbon seizure on the 1.4 Turbo (A14NET – Mokka A, Astra J); DPF/EGR clogging on the 1.6 CDTI (B16DTH – Astra K, Zafira C); and wet timing belt disintegration on the 1.2 PureTech (EB2ADTS – Corsa F, Grandland). These failures are often catastrophic – by the time symptoms appear, a full replacement is usually more cost‑effective than repair.
items.cta: See which Vauxhall engine codes fail most – and compare replacement costs

items.question: How can I get the best price on a Vauxhall engine?
items.answer: Always compare multiple quotes – prices on the same Vauxhall engine code can vary by £1,000+ between suppliers. Consider a used engine only for short‑term fixes; for long‑term ownership, a reconditioned unit with upgraded components costs more upfront but saves money on repeat repairs. Ask for supply‑only if you have a trusted independent garage (their labour rates are often 30–50% lower than main dealers). Always check whether the quote includes VAT, delivery, fitting, and the warranty period. Using our comparison tool gives you all this information upfront, so you can choose the best value option, not just the cheapest headline price.
